name: Aramaio Loop Trail
name_original: Orixol y Santikurutz, circular desde Etxaguen — Parque Natural de Urkiola
description: Hike the moderate 5.1-mile Orixol y Santikurutz circular route from Etxaguen, offering panoramic views in Urkiola Natural Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Aramaio offers diverse and mountainous natural landscapes in the heart of the Basque Country. The region is characterized by a mix of forests, peaks, and valleys, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. A significant natural feature is the Urkiola Natural Park, which includes prominent peaks like Orixol and Asentsiomendi. This area provides a backdrop for numerous hiking trails. Best hiking trails around Aramaio The most popular hiking route is Orixol y Santikurutz, circular desde Etxaguen — Parque Natural de Urkiola, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route is popular for its accessible path through the Urkiola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Larrano Puntea y Anboto por el Pagazeilaburu — circular por el Parque Natural de Urkiola, a difficult 9.8 miles (15.8 km) path. This challenging route offers views within the majestic Urkiola Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Beech Forest – Urkiola Sanctuary loop from Otxandio, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through a beautiful beech forest, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Aramaio is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and prominent peaks within the Urkiola Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Aramaio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Aramaio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aramaio, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Aramaio offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 5 easy, 17 moderate, and 15 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Aramaio?
A: Yes, Aramaio has several routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1388633280/orixol-y-santikurutz-circular-desde-etxaguen-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orixol y Santikurutz, circular desde Etxaguen&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 8.2 km trail takes around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete and is popular for its accessible path through the Urkiola Natural Park.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Aramaio?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Aramaio are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Besides the family-friendly option mentioned, another popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beech Forest – Urkiola Sanctuary loop from Otxandio&lt;/a&gt;, which guides hikers through a beautiful beech forest.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in Aramaio for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a more strenuous adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384125402/larrano-puntea-y-anboto-por-el-pagazeilaburu-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrano Puntea y Anboto por el Pagazeilaburu&lt;/a&gt; trail is an excellent choice. This difficult 15.8 km route offers challenging views within the majestic Urkiola Natural Park and typically takes nearly 7 hours to complete. It requires very good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Aramaio?
A: Aramaio's mountainous terrain offers a stunning variety of landscapes, including dense forests, prominent peaks, and serene valleys. A significant portion of the hiking takes place within the beautiful Urkiola Natural Park, known for its natural monuments and diverse ecosystems, including notable beech forests.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region around Aramaio is rich with natural and historical points of interest. You can explore fascinating natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eye of Aitzulo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3889987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baltzola Caves&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullíbarri-Gamboa Village and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic lakeside paths.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Aramaio?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Aramaio, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diversity of landscapes, the quality of the paths, and the stunning views, especially within the Urkiola Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aramaio?
A: While Aramaio offers hiking opportunities year-round, spring and autumn are particularly popular. During these seasons, the weather is generally mild, and the natural scenery, especially the beech forests, is at its most vibrant with lush greens or rich autumnal colors. Summer can also be pleasant, though some routes might be more exposed to the sun.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' trails in Aramaio?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by individual exploration, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santikurutz Hermitage – Orixol loop from Aramaio&lt;/a&gt; offers a slightly less frequented but equally rewarding experience. This moderate 7.4 km trail provides beautiful views and a sense of tranquility away from the most popular routes.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Aramaio?
A: Hiking durations in Aramaio vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. You can find shorter walks taking around 2-3 hours, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1388633280/orixol-y-santikurutz-circular-desde-etxaguen-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orixol y Santikurutz, circular desde Etxaguen&lt;/a&gt; (approx. 3h 27m). More challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384125402/larrano-puntea-y-anboto-por-el-pagazeilaburu-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-urkiola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrano Puntea y Anboto por el Pagazeilaburu&lt;/a&gt;, can take up to 7 hours or more.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to prominent peaks or viewpoints?
A: Yes, Aramaio's mountainous landscape means many trails offer stunning views and lead to prominent peaks. The Urkiola Natural Park, which is central to many hikes, features peaks like Orixol and Anboto. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10510588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santikurutz Hermitage – Orixol loop from Oleta&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent opportunities to reach elevated viewpoints and enjoy panoramic vistas.
tips: This beautiful and green hill is located between the peaks of Ipizte and Orixol, which are two very good options if you want to put a finishing touch to your excursion. Very close you can also find a cave-shelter in the rock, curious to see...
Great views. Be careful if you decide to go for the cut, because sometimes the stones are dangerous and there is some aerial zone.
Anteroom of the Orixol, and very close to the homonymous hermitage. Stunning views, as in the entire tailpiece.
You can reach this hermitage by continuing along the rocky crest between the peak of the same name and that of Kastillo.
With 1,036 meters of altitude, this terraced and grassy peak may take its name due to the strategic and defensive function with which it was used in the Carlist wars.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Orixol y Santikurutz circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike usually takes around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete. Keep in mind that this duration can vary based on your pace, the number of stops you make for photos, and your overall fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate. Hikers should be in good physical condition and have steady footing. The terrain is quite varied, including mountain trails, footpaths, and natural paths. As you ascend towards the summits of Orixol and Santikurutz, you'll encounter rocky stretches that can be challenging due to loose stones and some exposed areas. There's also a beautiful forested section as you descend towards the Lezeaga Pass.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park in Etxaguen?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Etxaguen. While specific parking details aren't provided, Etxaguen serves as the main access point for this route, and you should be able to find suitable parking within or near the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or summits will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich with notable features! You'll have the opportunity to conquer the peaks of Orixol and Santikurutz. Other key points of interest include the scenic Lezeaga Pass, the historic Santikurutz Hermitage, and potentially the summit of Kastillo, which retains traces of Civil War trenches.

faqs.question: What kind of panoramic views can I expect from the summits?
faqs.answer: From the summits, especially Orixol, you'll be rewarded with fantastic and spectacular panoramic views. You can admire vistas of Gorbea, the Amboto Massif, and Udalatx, offering a truly engaging visual experience throughout the hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Orixol y Santikurutz circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in many seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. During winter, be prepared for potentially challenging conditions, especially on higher, rocky sections, which may be covered in snow or ice.

faqs.question: Is the Orixol y Santikurutz trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including rocky and potentially exposed sections, ensure your dog is accustomed to such conditions and always keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for Urkiola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike this trail, and there is no entrance fee for Urkiola Natural Park. The park is freely accessible for visitors to enjoy its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Vuelta a Aramaio and the Euskal Herriko ibilaldia trails.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for navigating the trail or challenging sections?
faqs.answer: The trail is often marked as part of the PR A-21, using white and yellow signals, which can help with navigation. Pay extra attention on the rocky stretches towards the summits, as they can be challenging. Appropriate footwear with good grip is highly recommended for these sections.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Etxaguen after the hike?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is in a natural park, Etxaguen is a small village. You might find basic amenities there, but for a wider selection of cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you may need to venture to slightly larger towns nearby.
